name: Input
description: A versatile input component supporting text, textarea, prefix/suffix elements, various sizes, and validation states for form data entry.
usage_guidelines: Use for single-line text input in forms.
Set textArea prop for multi-line text input.
Use prefix/suffix for icons, labels, or units (e.g., currency symbols, search icons).
Set invalid prop to show error state with validation feedback.
Choose appropriate type for input purpose (text, email, password, number, etc.).

prefix.description: Element displayed at the start of input (icons, labels).

rows.description: Number of visible rows for textarea.

suffix.description: Element displayed at the end of input (icons, units).

textArea.description: Renders as textarea for multi-line input.

type.description: HTML input type (text, email, password, number, etc.).

unstyle.description: Removes default styling for custom implementations.

readOnly.description: Makes input read-only (visible but not editable).

styling_info.visual_description: A rectangular input field with rounded corners, border, and focus ring. Prefix/suffix elements are positioned inside the input with appropriate spacing. Invalid state shows red border and focus ring. Disabled state has reduced opacity.

Do: Use appropriate input type for data validation
Use prefix/suffix for contextual icons or units
Set invalid state with validation messages
Dont: Don't use for complex selections (use Select instead)
Don't forget to handle controlled value properly
Don't use both value and defaultValue
Don't make inputs too wide or too narrow for expected content
